content.text:
{
  "dimension": "perceptual-accuracy",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "Joe's work here is brilliant on intervention and stance, but on the specific dimension of visible reads — naming how the client is showing up somatically, energetically, or linguistically — there is relatively little in the text. He dissolves the frame and inverts the question rather than naming what he notices about her state. The closest things to reads are implicit: at turn 6 he picks up on the self-judging stance ('Who are you to judge if your input is valuable?'), and at turn 8 he names a pattern ('If you think you have to be important, that's a selfishness of itself') which functions as a self-importance-as-defense read. But these are framed as challenges/teachings rather than as observations of how she's showing up in the room. He does not name her body, pace, tone, or the question-as-defense move explicitly. When she smiles at turn 9, he doesn't reflect it; when she says 'it feels ridiculous' at turn 13, he doesn't track the somatic shift, he closes. The embodied experiment at turn 12 is set up as an experiment, not as a read of something he's already noticing. This is solidly a 2: some pattern-level reads are operative beneath the surface, but reads are not explicitly named in a way that visibly anchors the questions.",
  "evidence_turns": [6, 8, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Inverts the question rather than naming a read — strong move but no visible observation of how she's showing up."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Implicit read of the self-judging/scorekeeping stance ('Who are you to judge'), but framed as challenge rather than named observation."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names self-importance-as-defense at a pattern level ('If you think you have to be important, that's a selfishness of itself') — operative read, though not framed as 'I notice'."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Pivots to control-seeking driver without naming the somatic shift in turn 9 (her smile, her recognition)."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Sets up embodied experiment but not anchored in an explicit read of what he's seeing in her body or stance."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Closes without naming the shift he's tracking — the laughter/ridiculousness lands but is not reflected back as a read."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["self-importance-as-defense"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["looking-for-whats-wrong", "the-question-as-defense"],
  "notes": "The conversation is excellent on other dimensions (intervention, stance, anti-pattern avoidance) but this dimension specifically asks about visible reads named in text. Joe's reads are mostly operative-but-unnamed; he goes straight to the move. Text-only scoring constraint applies."
}
